proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age (age_years) is associated with shorter pfs_months in this NSCLC cohort.
Female patients (sex_female=1) have shorter pfs_months than male patients.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate OLS: each additional year of age is associated with +0.170 months PFS (p<1e-300). The direction is opposite to the prior hypothesis: older patients in this cohort have LONGER PFS.
Mean PFS 3.32 mo (female) vs 3.52 mo (male); difference -0.204 mo, p=7.7e-25. Hypothesis supported: women have shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Current smokers have shorter pfs_months than former or never smokers (smoking_status='current' lower than 'former'/'never').

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: never 3.68, former 3.61, current 2.99. ANOVA F=433, p=3.7e-187. Current smokers have markedly shorter PFS; never and former are nearly identical.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (worse performance status → worse PFS).
stage_iv=1 (metastatic) is associated with shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0.
has_brain_mets=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0.

analyses.result_summary: Each ECOG point reduces PFS by 1.099 months (p<1e-300). Strongly supported.
Stage IV vs non–stage IV: difference -1.438 months PFS, p<1e-300. Strongly supported.
Brain mets vs no brain mets: -0.921 months PFS, p<1e-300. Supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with adenocarcinoma histology have longer pfs_months than patients with squamous histology.

analyses.result_summary: Adenocarcinoma mean PFS 3.66 vs squamous 2.81 (difference +0.857 mo, p<1e-300). Supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: egfr_mutation=1 patients have longer pfs_months than EGFR-wildtype patients.
kras_g12c=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than kras_g12c=0 (KRAS-driven tumors typically have worse prognosis).
alk_fusion=1 patients have longer pfs_months than alk_fusion=0 (driver alteration with available targeted therapy).

analyses.result_summary: EGFR+ mean 3.60 vs EGFR- 3.41; +0.189 mo, p=6.5e-11. Supported: EGFR+ have modestly longer PFS.
KRAS G12C+ mean 4.11 vs KRAS- 3.33; difference +0.78 mo, p=7e-94. OPPOSITE of hypothesis: KRAS G12C+ patients have LONGER PFS in this cohort. Likely because many receive sotorasib (see iter 13).

ALK+ mean 2.66 vs ALK- 3.47; difference -0.81 mo, p=5.6e-66. OPPOSITE of hypothesis: in this cohort, ALK fusion is associated with markedly SHORTER PFS (no ALK inhibitor is among the available treatments).

proposed_hypotheses.text: stk11_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months (STK11/LKB1 loss is a known poor-prognostic marker).
brca2_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months in untargeted population.
tmb_high=1 is associated with longer pfs_months (high TMB usually predicts immunotherapy benefit and reflects DNA-repair deficiency).
Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: STK11+ vs STK11-: +0.009 mo, p=0.74. NOT supported: no marginal effect of STK11 on PFS in this cohort.
BRCA2+ vs BRCA2-: -0.119 mo, p=0.031. Marginally supported (small effect).
TMB high mean 3.30 vs TMB low 3.49; -0.190 mo, p=1.0e-17. OPPOSITE of hypothesis: TMB high is associated with SHORTER PFS, suggesting more aggressive disease without effective targeted/IO match.
PD-L1 TPS coefficient +0.067 per unit (range 0–0.8), p=0.16. Not significant marginally.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Slope +0.441 mo per g/dL albumin (p=2.8e-109). Strongly supported.
Slope -0.00051 mo per U/L LDH (p=3.6e-5). Supported, small per-unit but meaningful across LDH range.
Slope -0.073 mo per percentage point weight loss (p=7.5e-179). Strongly supported.
CRP slope +0.0003 (p=0.78). NOT supported marginally.
NLR slope +0.0039 (p=0.42). NOT supported marginally.

proposed_hypotheses.text: None of hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl have a marginal association with pfs_months at p<0.05.

analyses.result_summary: p-values: hemoglobin 0.75, alk phos 0.14, AST 0.70, ALT 0.20, bilirubin 0.41, creatinine 0.22, BUN 0.51, Na 0.48, K 0.33, Ca 0.29. All NS marginally — no liver/renal/electrolyte panel value moves PFS on its own.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall vs untreated patients.
treatment_sotorasib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
treatment_olaparib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
treatment_osimertinib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.

analyses.result_summary: Pembro 3.41 vs control 3.45 → -0.035 mo, p=0.081. NOT supported overall: pembrolizumab has no marginal benefit (slight numerical detriment).
Sotorasib 3.64 vs control 3.32 → +0.322 mo, p=5.6e-49. Supported overall, large effect (driven entirely by KRAS G12C+ subgroup, see iter 13).
Olaparib 3.41 vs control 3.44 → -0.025 mo, p=0.26. NOT supported: no overall PFS benefit from olaparib in this cohort.
Osimertinib 3.434 vs control 3.431 → +0.004 mo, p=0.87. NOT supported: no overall PFS benefit from osimertinib (surprising given EGFR+ patients are present).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with pdl1_tps>=0.5 (PD-L1 high), treatment_pembrolizumab=1 yields longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0; the interaction treatment_pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps is positive.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.116 (p=0.22). In PD-L1 high subgroup pembro mean 3.41 vs 3.48 (-0.07, p=0.11); in PD-L1 low pembro 3.42 vs 3.44 (-0.025, p=0.26). NOT supported — pembrolizumab does not benefit PD-L1 high patients in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among tmb_high=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab=1 yields longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0; the treatment_pembrolizumab × tmb_high interaction is positive.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.018 (p=0.68). In TMB high pembro 3.27 vs 3.32 (-0.048, p=0.20); in TMB low pembro 3.47 vs 3.50 (-0.030, p=0.20). NOT supported — pembrolizumab benefit is not amplified by high TMB.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among stk11_mutation=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab=1 has reduced or no benefit relative to wildtype; the treatment_pembrolizumab × stk11_mutation interaction is negative.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.016 (p=0.77). STK11+: pembro 3.42 vs 3.46 (-0.049, p=0.34); STK11-: pembro 3.41 vs 3.45 (-0.032, p=0.14). NOT supported — STK11 status does not modify pembrolizumab effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among kras_g12c=1 patients, treatment_sotorasib=1 yields longer pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib=0 (positive treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = +2.558 (p<1e-300). In KRAS G12C+ patients: sotorasib mean 5.77 vs control 3.21 (+2.55 mo, p=1.7e-202; n=2246 vs 4122). In KRAS G12C-: -0.005 mo, p=0.80. STRONGLY SUPPORTED — overwhelmingly the largest treatment effect in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib=1 yields longer pfs_months than treatment_olaparib=0 (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = +0.037 (p=0.78). BRCA2+ patients: olaparib 3.32 vs 3.31 (+0.010, p=0.93; n=417 vs 1102). BRCA2-: -0.026 (p=0.24). NOT supported — olaparib does not appear to benefit BRCA2+ NSCLC in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among egfr_mutation=1 patients, treatment_osimertinib=1 yields longer pfs_months than treatment_osimertinib=0 (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.020 (p=0.76). EGFR+: osimertinib 3.59 vs 3.60 (-0.014, p=0.81; n=1973 vs 4544). EGFR-: +0.006 (p=0.81). NOT supported — osimertinib has no detectable PFS benefit in EGFR+ patients in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS of pfs_months on all clinical/biomarker/lab features (no treatments) explains a substantial share of variance (R^2>0.5), with the largest |coefficients| coming from stage_iv, ecog_ps, has_brain_mets, smoking_status, histology, kras_g12c (positive in this cohort), albumin_g_dl, sex_female, and age_years.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age + sex + C(smoking) + ecog + C(hist) + stage_iv + brain + 5 muts + pdl1 + tmb + 12 labs', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: R^2 = 0.889. Largest |coef|: stage_iv -1.42, ecog_ps -1.10, has_brain_mets -0.91, kras_g12c +0.89, squamous -0.72, former-smoker +0.55, never-smoker +0.55, albumin +0.46, sex_female -0.21, age +0.168/yr, brca2 -0.11, weight_loss -0.073, alk_fusion -0.030 (NS), stk11 -0.020 (p=0.03). Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding the four treatments and their key biomarker interactions (pembro×pdl1_tps, pembro×tmb_high, pembro×stk11_mutation, sotorasib×kras_g12c, olaparib×brca2_mutation, osimertinib×egfr_mutation, osimertinib×has_brain_mets) to the multivariable model materially improves R^2, and only the sotorasib×kras_g12c interaction remains significant.

analyses.result_summary: R^2 increases from 0.889 to 0.927. Among the treatment-related coefficients, only treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c is significant: beta=+2.535 (p<1e-300). Pembro and its interactions: |beta|<0.003, p>0.87. Olaparib and olaparib:brca2: NS. Osimertinib and osimertinib:egfr: NS (p=0.21). Osimertinib×has_brain_mets: beta=+0.026, p=0.055 (borderline). SUPPORTED — sotorasib×KRAS G12C dominates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A treatment_sotorasib × {sex_female, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, egfr_mutation, kras_g12c, alk_fusion, stk11_mutation, brca2_mutation, tmb_high, pdl1_high, smoking_status, histology} interaction screen will identify kras_g12c as by far the largest modifier; other apparent significant interactions (sex, smoking, alk_fusion, egfr, brca2) reflect the lower frequency of kras_g12c in those subgroups rather than independent effect modification.

analyses.code: for each modifier: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ trt_sotorasib * mod', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Largest interactions (signed beta, p): kras_g12c +2.558 (p~0); sex_female -0.562 (p=5.3e-41); never-smoker -0.497 (p=3.1e-14); alk_fusion -0.488 (p=4.1e-7); former-smoker -0.211 (p=6.9e-6); egfr_mutation -0.241 (p=9.9e-5); squamous +0.171 (p=1.9e-4); brca2 -0.334 (p=6.1e-3). All negative non-KRAS modifiers reflect lower KRAS G12C prevalence in those strata; in KRAS G12C+ patients alone the sotorasib effect (+2.55–2.59 mo) is uniform. SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A treatment_pembrolizumab × {biomarker} interaction screen across the same modifier list will not identify any modifier with p<0.01 — pembrolizumab has no detectable subgroup with a positive PFS effect at conventional thresholds.

analyses.code: for each modifier: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ trt_pembro * mod', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Smallest interaction p-values: stage_iv +0.108 (p=6.8e-3); kras_g12c -0.113 (p=0.057); egfr +0.099 (p=0.10); all others p>0.13. No modifier reaches a meaningful subgroup-specific benefit. SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Heterogeneity scans for treatment_olaparib and treatment_osimertinib will not identify any modifier with p<0.01; in particular brca2_mutation and egfr_mutation will not modify their respective treatment effects.

analyses.code: for each modifier: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ trt * mod', df).fit() for olaparib and osimertinib
analyses.result_summary: Olaparib: no interaction p<0.05 (smallest stk11 0.073, squamous 0.106). Osimertinib: smallest is alk_fusion +0.238 (p=0.017) and brca2 -0.254 (p=0.050) — both small and likely chance given multiplicity. egfr_mutation × osimertinib: -0.020, p=0.76. SUPPORTED — neither olaparib nor osimertinib has a meaningful subgroup of benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within kras_g12c=1, stk11_mutation status does NOT meaningfully modify the sotorasib effect (the sotorasib×kras_g12c×stk11 three-way interaction p>0.05).
The maximally enriched subgroup for sotorasib benefit is kras_g12c=1 alone; restricting further to stk11_mutation=0 yields effectively the same effect size (~+2.55 to +2.59 mo).

analyses.result_summary: 3-way interaction beta=-0.271, p=0.128 (NS). KRAS+/STK11-wt sotorasib: 5.79 vs 3.20 (+2.59 mo, p=3.2e-179, n=1959/3513). KRAS+/STK11-mut: 5.61 vs 3.28 (+2.33, p=3.6e-25, n=287/609). STK11 does NOT functionally suppress sotorasib in this cohort. SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within pdl1_tps>=0.5 patients, the three-way interaction treatment_pembrolizumab × tmb_high × stk11_mutation will not yield a clinically meaningful or statistically robust subgroup of pembrolizumab benefit.
The compound subgroup (pdl1_high=1 AND tmb_high=1 AND stk11_mutation=0) does not show a positive pembrolizumab effect at p<0.05.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ trt_pembro*tmb_high*stk11_mutation', df.loc[pdl1_high==1]).fit(); ttest in compound
analyses.result_summary: Within PD-L1 high (n≈10,750): pembro main beta=-0.156 (p=0.005), pembro:tmb_high beta=+0.212 (p=0.040), pembro:stk11 beta=+0.167 (p=0.24), 3-way beta=+0.025 (p=0.93). Compound subgroup pdl1_high & tmb_high & stk11_wt: pembro 3.33 vs 3.28 (+0.056, p=0.52, n=1373/1341). Same compound but stk11_mut: +0.248, p=0.25 (n=217/219). NOT supported — no robust pembrolizumab benefit subgroup; the only nominally negative pembro effect is in PD-L1 high & TMB low patients (~-0.10 to -0.16 mo).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The three-way interaction treatment_osimertinib × egfr_mutation × has_brain_mets is not significant — osimertinib does not preferentially extend PFS in EGFR+ patients with brain metastases.

analyses.result_summary: Three-way beta=+0.056, p=0.70. Subgroup means: EGFR+/brain+ osimertinib 2.95 vs 2.90 (+0.051, p=0.64); EGFR+/brain- 3.80 vs 3.83 (-0.029, p=0.66); EGFR-/brain+ 2.73 vs 2.71 (+0.022, p=0.62); EGFR-/brain- 3.64 vs 3.64 (NS). NO subgroup of osimertinib benefit. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment effects stratified by ecog_ps will preserve the sotorasib benefit (in the KRAS-mixed treated group) across ECOG levels and will not reveal benefit for any other treatment in any ECOG stratum.

analyses.code: trt_effect for each treatment within ecog_ps=0,1,2
analyses.result_summary: Sotorasib (overall, mix of KRAS+/-): ECOG 0 +0.31 (p=6e-18); ECOG 1 +0.31 (p=7e-27); ECOG 2 +0.30 (p=2e-9). Pembro: ECOG 0 +0.031 (p=0.35); ECOG 1 -0.066 (p=0.013, small); ECOG 2 -0.010 (p=0.82). Olaparib: all NS. Osimertinib: all NS. SUPPORTED — sotorasib effect (driven by KRAS G12C+) is robust across ECOG; no other treatment has subgroup benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL pembrolizumab subgroup hypothesis: there is NO subgroup, including (pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND tmb_high=1 AND stk11_mutation=0), in which treatment_pembrolizumab=1 produces a statistically significant longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0 in this cohort. Direction is essentially null/slightly negative.

FINAL sotorasib subgroup hypothesis: in patients with kras_g12c=1, treatment_sotorasib=1 produces substantially longer pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib=0 (~+2.55 months, ~80% longer median PFS). Restricting further by stk11_mutation=0 does not materially change the effect (+2.59 mo); KRAS G12C+ alone is the operative subgroup definition.
FINAL olaparib subgroup hypothesis: no subgroup, including brca2_mutation=1, shows a significant positive treatment_olaparib effect on pfs_months in this cohort.

FINAL osimertinib subgroup hypothesis: no subgroup, including egfr_mutation=1 and egfr_mutation=1 AND has_brain_mets=1, shows a significant positive treatment_osimertinib effect on pfs_months in this cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Compound subgroup PD-L1 high & TMB high & STK11-wt (n=1373 vs 1341): pembro 3.33 vs 3.28, +0.056 mo, p=0.52. NOT supported. No subgroup of pembrolizumab benefit found.

KRAS G12C+ overall (n=2246 vs 4122): sotorasib 5.77 vs 3.21, +2.55 mo, p=1.7e-202. KRAS+/STK11-wt strict (n=1959 vs 3513): +2.59 mo, p=3.2e-179. The strict subgroup is essentially equivalent — KRAS G12C+ alone is the operative subgroup. STRONGLY SUPPORTED.
BRCA2+ (n=417 vs 1102): olaparib 3.32 vs 3.31, +0.010 mo, p=0.93. Heterogeneity scan p_min=0.073. NOT supported.
EGFR+ (n=1973 vs 4544): osimertinib 3.59 vs 3.60, -0.014, p=0.81. EGFR+ & brain+ (n=503 vs 1132): +0.051, p=0.64. NOT supported.
